idea-plugin.name: Nexus Framework
idea-plugin.description:
IDE support for the nexus Go framework:

  • YAML linting for nexus.deploy.yaml — every issue nexus lint --yaml would surface is shown inline in the editor.
  • Run configurations for nexus dev and nexus lint so the framework's dev loop is a one-click action.
  • Tool window showing the running app's registered plugins + endpoints, fetched from /__nexus/plugins + /__nexus/endpoints.
  • Live template (.nlt) support — HTML editor for nexus SFCs plus autocomplete for nl-* directives, shorthand :/@/#, and common event names.

Requires the nexus CLI binary on PATH (or configured via Settings → Tools → Nexus). Built against the framework's v0.43.x line.
idea-plugin.change-notes:
0.7.2 — field completion inside call-form event handler args. Typing @click="like(Post.|" now offers the Post struct's fields (ID / Title / Author / Likes) instead of the component's handler names. ExpressionContext slices the comma-separated arg containing the caret out of the call expression and reports it as an attribute-value context, so the existing scope-based chain resolver picks up the same way it does for :foo="Post.|". Top-level commas / nested parens / string literals are honored when slicing.

0.7.1 — call-form event handlers (@click="like(Post.ID)") now get the same NexusExpr syntax highlighting as the other expression attributes — keywords, idents, operators, parens, dots all paint distinctly instead of the value reading as one opaque string. Cmd+B on the handler name still resolves to the Go method even when the value is a call expression (strips the args before lookup). Method-name matching also stopped requiring Payload in the Go signature so the new typed form func (c *T) Like(ctx *Ctx, id int) surfaces in completion and navigation.

0.7.0 — quick-doc panels for nl-*, :, @, # attributes in .nlt files. Hover (or Ctrl+Q) shows what the directive does, when to use it, and an inline example. Covers every directive currently recognized by the engine — structural (nl-if, nl-for, etc.), client (nl-hook, nl-stream, nl-navigate), prefix shorthand (:foo, @click, #slot), and the modifier-bearing event forms.

0.6.2 — Cmd+B on an @event handler now skips empty stub methods when a real implementation exists elsewhere. So in PostRow.nlt's @click="like", Cmd+B lands directly on PostsList.Like (the bubbled parent handler with a real body) instead of opening a picker that includes the empty PostRow.Like stub.

0.6.1 — Cmd+B on an @event handler name now also finds the method on any parent component that hosts this one. Events bubble — the local component's same-named method is often an empty stub while the real handler lives on the parent. Local component is still searched first; remote components add candidates IntelliJ presents in a picker when there are multiple matches.

0.6.0 — three .nlt editor improvements:

  • Type-aware completion now fires inside attribute values again. v0.5.0's NexusExpr injection moved the caret PSI out of the host XmlAttributeValue subtree, breaking field completion on :data-id="Post.|" and similar. The completion contributor now resolves back through InjectedLanguageManager to the host PSI before computing scope.
  • Handler-name completion on @click="|" and nl-on:event="|" offers the methods on the local component struct whose signature looks like an event handler (takes template.Payload) — filters out lifecycle methods like Mount and Refresh.
  • Cmd+B from template to Go source. Cursor on a bare ident (Posts), a chained ident (Post.ID), or an event handler name (like) jumps to the field or method declaration in the matching Go file. Backed by an extended GoScanner that now captures per-field and per-method file offsets.

0.5.0 — code-style highlighting inside attribute expressions. The value of nl-for, nl-if, nl-model, :foo, etc. is now lexed as a small "NexusExpr" language: keywords (true/false/nil/in/len), identifiers, numbers, strings, operators, and dots get their own colours matching the platform's default scheme. Same approach Vue uses for JS expressions in attribute values, scaled to our smaller surface.

0.4.2 — finishes the warning-suppression story. Adds a daemon HighlightInfoFilter that catches the "Empty tag doesn't work in some browsers" warning (emitted by an Annotator, not an Inspection, so it escapes the v0.4.1 suppressor). Same scope rules: .nlt files only.

0.4.1 — three .nlt polish fixes:

  • Cmd+B on <PostRow /> now finds post_row.nlt (snake_case), postRow.nlt (camelCase), and post-row.nlt (kebab-case), not just PostRow.nlt.
  • Suppresses HTML inspections that flag legitimate SFC constructs in .nlt files: "Unknown HTML tag" on PascalCase component refs, "Empty tag doesn't work in some browsers" on self-closed components, and "Attribute scoped is not allowed here" on <style scoped>.
  • Expression scope now actually consults the Go registration index when picking a component name — posts.nlt resolves to Posts only when Posts is truly registered, instead of guessing blindly.

0.4.0 — type-aware expression completion. Typing {{ post.| }} or :data-id="post.|" in a .nlt file now offers the actual exported fields of the bound Go struct, walking dotted chains like post.Author.Name through field types as you go. Loop variables from nl-for="x in Xs" resolve to the element type of Xs and become first-class scope names.

Implementation regex-scans project Go files for engine.Register(…) / template.RegisterComponent(…) registrations and type T struct {…} declarations — no Go plugin dependency, no build-classpath churn, degrades silently when a type can't be resolved.

0.3.0 — .nlt polish. Three fixes/additions: autocomplete now matches multi-character prefixes (nl-, @click, :src) using a literal PlainPrefixMatcher; framework attributes are whitelisted with the HTML inspector so @submit.prevent / nl-model no longer flash "not allowed here" warnings; and Cmd+B / Cmd+Click on a <PascalCase /> component tag jumps to the matching {TagName}.nlt file in the project.

0.2.0 — .nlt (nexus live template SFC) support. Files with the .nlt extension open in the HTML editor (highlighting, attribute completion, structure view, brace matching) and a new completion contributor adds framework directives — nl-if, nl-for, nl-model, nl-bind:, nl-on:, shorthand :/@/#, and the common DOM event names the runtime delegates.

0.1.0 — initial release. YAML linting via the nexus lint --yaml --json CLI, two run configurations, and a tool window for the running app's plugins/endpoints.
